ExtraProperties.Text:
Overview

All SnuggPro recommended units for improvement must meet minimum measure standards. Projects require a Savings-to-Investment Ratio (SIR) of 0.5 or higher to be approved. Corrections will be sent to the contractor's point of contact.

Project Status Definitions

In Progress: Currently being reviewed

Archive Lost: Customer no longer interested in auditable measures, the program, or project doesn't meet SIR requirements

Pending Corrections: Corrections needed on SnuggPro, status set to Uncategorized

Approved: Project passes with 0.5 SIR or higher, approved for auditable measures. SnuggPro status set to Bid Approved

Approved—Waiver: Project approved but requires waiver due to total project cost. SnuggPro status set to Bid Approved

Project Notes 

In the project notes section, add any major updates or changes to the audit. For all Unit specification changes, please upload the new AHRI provided.

Change Request: Any changes to Specs of units or removal of a measure from project after approval.

Project Update: Any measures removed or any changes to the project after approval

Customer Information Section

  • Enrollment Number: Tied to customer IMPACT account, auto-populates customer name and primary contractor
  • SnuggPro Project Number: Found in SnuggPro under "Job Information" tab
  • Audit Date: Auto-populates to form creation date
  • Approved Date: Auto-populates when "Approved" or "Approved—Waiver" is selected
  • Project SIR: Located in SnuggPro REFINE section (top right)

Section-by-Section Review Requirements

1. Building Section

Requirements: All fields are required on SnuggPro. Review information based on assessment form and online resources.

Review Checklist:

  • Verify building length, width, and height using online websites
  • Confirm address and year built using online websites
  • Verify conditioned area based on assessment form and online websites
  • Confirm home orientation (North, East, South, West) using Maps—based on door to street
  • Verify number of bedrooms and bathrooms using online websites
  • Confirm dwelling type (single-family, multi-family, mobile home)
  • Verify shielding type using maps and SnuggPro help text

1. Utilty Bills

Requirements: Utilty provider names need to be inputted. Bill entry type will always be "No bills"

Review Checklist:

  • Bill Entry Type
  • Utility provider names
  • Click on " Manual Utility Pricing & Settings", input Electricity Price and Natural Gas Price based on current average utilty rates.

2. Thermostat

Requirements: All fields required. Review base inputs from provided pictures. Improved inputs use default values for customer education.

Photos Required: 1 heating and 1 cooling set to "On"

Base Section

  • Check for programmable thermostat in submitted photos
  • Review and confirm existing temperature setpoints in pictures

Improved Section (Default Values)

  • Heat High: 68°F
  • Heat Low: 65°F
  • Cool Low: 78°F
  • Cool High: 80°F

3. HVAC System

Requirements: All base fields required. Verify heating/cooling load based on number of systems in home.

Photos Required: 1 unit picture and 1 data plate picture

For Upgrades/Replacements: AHRI certificate of new model required in SnuggPro

Base Section

  • Verify model number matches data plate in pictures
  • Confirm unit information corresponds to model number specifications

Improved Section

  • Confirm improved unit model number matches AHRI certificate
  • Verify improved unit specifications match AHRI data

4. Appliances and Refrigerator

Requirements: All base fields required. Verify information from data plate and model number.

Photos Required: Existing unit picture and existing data plate picture

For Upgrades: Energy Star certificates required

Base Section

  • Verify existing appliance model numbers match data plate pictures
  • Confirm existing appliances are not Energy Star certified

Improved Section

  • Check Measure Installation Request (MIR) in IMPACT system for appliance upgrades
  • Ensure SnuggPro inputs match MIR requests
  • For upgrades, verify improved unit specifications using Energy Star certificate

5. Lighting

Requirements: All base fields required. LED replacement quantity reflects in LED section.

Base Section

  • Verify existing lightbulb types (LED, CFL, or Incandescent) using pictures
  • Confirm base inputs include correct quantity and type throughout home

Improved Section

  • Verify requested lightbulb quantity matches MIR
  • If no lighting improvements, improved section must mirror base input
  • Example: If base has 2 LED bulbs, improved section should also have 2 LED bulbs

6. Doors

Requirements: All base fields required. Verify door type from pictures and confirm SQFT matches door type.

Base Section

  • Confirm number of exterior doors using provided photos
  • Verify door SQFT is accurately entered based on images

Improved Section

  • Improved door inputs should mirror base values unless upgrade is performed

7. Walls

Requirements: Calculate actual wall area by subtracting door and window SQFT from total wall area. This calculation is required for every project.

Base Section

  • Use pictures to determine if wall insulation is present
  • Calculate actual wall area using formula:
(Height × (Width + Length)) × 2
- Door SQFT
- Window SQFT
= Actual Wall Area

Improved Section

  • Improved wall square footage must match base wall square footage
  • If wall insulation not being upgraded, improved input should mirror base values

8. Attic

Requirements: All base fields required.

Photos Required: Ruler measuring insulation. If there are noticeable high and low spots, upload two photos—one showing a high spot and one showing a low spot.

Base Section

  • Verify existing insulation type and depth using submitted pictures

Improved Section

  • If attic insulation improved, confirm type and R-value meet Title 24 requirements

9. Foundation

Base Section

  • Confirm foundation type correctly marked as 100% for either basement, crawlspace, or slab

Improved Section

  • If basement or crawlspace selected, verify photos support the inputs

10. Windows

Requirements: All base fields required. Verify inputs based on IMPACT window form.

Base Section

  • Verify total window area using Assessment Form page 9
  • Confirm system type as single pane or double pane
  • Enter double pane and single pane windows as separate systems
  • Verify overhang depth using submitted images
  • Window venting in base section must be marked as "no"
  • Confirm window area using total window SQFT divided by wall SQFT per side of home
  • Ensure window quantities entered on correct side (North, East, South, West)

Improved Section

  • Window venting must be marked as "yes" regardless of improvements
  • If windows being improved, mark as "yes"; if not, mark as "no"
  • Improved window area must match base input
  • Note: Double pane windows cannot be upgraded—only single pane windows are eligible

11. Water Heater

Requirements: All base fields required.

Photos Required: Existing unit and data plate

For Improvements: AHRI certification required

Base Section

  • Verify water temperature matches value in submitted photo
  • Confirm model number input matches data plate picture
  • Verify specification inputs align with model number information

Improved Section

  • Water temperature can only be improved by up to 20°F, minimum 120°F
  • Verify improved model number matches AHRI documentation
  • Confirm improved specification inputs match AHRI certificate

12. Final SnuggPro Report Review

Final Steps:

  • Notify contractors of any corrections or missing data
  • Verify all approved measures in SnuggPro match MIR requests
  • Ensure all pricing in SnuggPro corresponds with approved upgrades
  • Download and upload final SnuggPro report to appropriate system

Key Reminders

  • SIR Requirement: Minimum 0.5 for approval
  • Photo Documentation: Required for all relevant sections
  • AHRI Certificates: Required for all HVAC and water heater upgrades
  • Energy Star Certificates: Required for appliance upgrades
  • Wall Area Calculation: Must be verified for every project
  • Window Upgrades: Only single pane windows eligible for improvement
  • LHR: Add LHR sheet to SnuggPro Final section. Add total price to measure LHR is supporting
  • New Circuit: Add total price of circuit to measure it will support
  • Lower Hot water Temp: Lower hot water can only be used if the project needs help passing the minimum .5 SIR. Price will be $50 minimum or $100 max and will be removed from another measure.

    • EXAMPLE- Water Heater $1,987 - $50= $1,937 for water heater on Snuggpro

ExtraProperties.Name: BuildingHelpText
ExtraProperties.Text:
• Verify Length, Width, & Height from Assessment Form 02
  • Verify Address & Year Built
  • Verify Conditioned Area
  • Verify House Orientation
  • Verify # of Bedrooms & Bathrooms
  • Verify Dwelling Type
  • Verify Shielding Type

ExtraProperties.Label: Building
ExtraProperties.Name: Building
ExtraProperties.HelpText: Required Photo- Screenshot of verified address that includes Address, number of rooms, SQFT, and year built

ExtraProperties.Name: AtticHelp
ExtraProperties.Text:
Base
  • Verify Existing insulation type and depth based off picture provided
Improved
  • If being improved-Verify correct insulation type and Rvalue matches based on Title 24 requirements

ExtraProperties.Label: Attic
ExtraProperties.Name: Attic
ExtraProperties.HelpText: Required photos-Ruler insulation picture, See page 2 for more info

ExtraProperties.Name: WallHelp
ExtraProperties.Text:
Base

  • Verify If wall Insulation is present based off picture provided

  • Verify Actual wall area

(Height x Width) +( Height x Length) x2 – Door SQFT – Total window SQFT= ACTUAL WALL SQFT
Improved
  • Wall SQFT matches Base SQFT
  • If not being improved, Verify section mirrors base section

ExtraProperties.Name: Walls
ExtraProperties.Label: Walls
ExtraProperties.HelpText: Required photos- 4 exterior pictures of each side of home

ExtraProperties.Name: DoorHelp
ExtraProperties.Text:
Base
  • Verify exterior door quantity based on pictures provided
  • Verify door SQFT input matches based on pictures
  • U-Value portion can be auto populated by SnuggPro for base and improved
Improved
  • Inputs will mirror base inputs

ExtraProperties.Name: Doors
ExtraProperties.Label: Doors
ExtraProperties.HelpText: Required photos-One Picture of each whole Door

ExtraProperties.Name: DuctsHelp
ExtraProperties.Text:
Base
  • All fields Required
    • Duct location, Leakage, and insulation
Improved
  • If no improvement- make sure status shows "No improvement"
  • If improving, only can improve too 10% leakage.
  • Reference duct calculator for correct duct seal amount (if needed)

ExtraProperties.Name: Ducts
ExtraProperties.Label: Ducts
ExtraProperties.HelpText: Required Photos- Pictures of condition of ducts and picture of gauge/reader

ExtraProperties.Name: AppliancesandRefrigeratorHelp
ExtraProperties.Text:
Base
  • Verify Existing Model number matches Data plate picture
  • Verify existing appliances are not Energy Star
Improved
    • Check MIR on IMPACT to see if they are requesting appliance upgrades
    • Verify Snuggpro matches what is being requested
    • If being Improved—Verify improved unit specs based on Energy star certificate

ExtraProperties.Label: Appliances and Refrigerator
ExtraProperties.Name: AppliancesandRefrigerator
ExtraProperties.HelpText: Required photos-Unit picture ,Data Plate Picture, and Energy star certificate

ExtraProperties.Name: HVACHelp
ExtraProperties.Text:
Base
  • Verify Existing Model number input matches Data plate picture
  • Verify Existing Unit information based on model Number
Improved
  • Verify Improved Unit Model number matches AHRI cert. provided
  • Verify Improved Unit inputs based on AHRI

ExtraProperties.Label: HVAC
ExtraProperties.Name: HVAC
ExtraProperties.HelpText: Required photo- Unit Picture and Data plate picture

ExtraProperties.Name: LightingHelp
ExtraProperties.Text:
Base
  • Verify Existing lightbulbs or CFL or Incandescent based on pictures
  • Base inputs need to reflect quantity of lightbulbs in home and type
Improved
  • Verify count of requested lightbulbs reflects MIR
  • If no improvements, LED inputs will be mirrored
  • Example:
    • Base=2 LED
      Improved=2 LED
  • If improving, Total light bulbs requested in improved LED section, not including existing LED
  • Example:
    CFL-5                  IMPROVED 0
    LED-10                IMPROVED 5
    Incandescent 0  IMPROVED 0

ExtraProperties.Label: Lighting
ExtraProperties.Name: Lighting
ExtraProperties.HelpText: Required photos-All existing Lighting in Home

ExtraProperties.Name: ThermostatHelp
ExtraProperties.Text:
Base
  • Verify if there is a programable thermostat
  • Verify existing setpoints based on pictures provided 
Improved 
  • Input the improved (new) setpoints
    • Heat High- 68
    • Heat Low-65
    • Cool Low-78
    • Cool High-80
  • If actual thermostat set points cannot be determined via the unit or by the customer, the thermostat set points shall be modeled using the new setpoints for base and improved

ExtraProperties.Label: Thermostat
ExtraProperties.Name: Thermostat
ExtraProperties.HelpText: Required Photo- Neutral State, Cool On, Heat On

ExtraProperties.Text:
Base
  • Verify Either basement, Crawl, or slab is marked at 100%

Improved
  • Verify Photos if basement or crawlspace is checked to 100%
ExtraProperties.Name: FoundationHelp
WindowsHelp
ExtraProperties.Text:
Base
  • Verify window area Based on IMPACT Assessment Form page 9
  • Verify system type (Single pane or double pane)
    • Note-Double pane and single pane need to be split into 2 systems
  • Verify Overhang depth matches based on pictures submitted
  • Window Venting Base is NO
  • Verify Window area matches IMPACT form 
  • Verify window amounts is inputted in the correct side of home (North, East, South, West)
Improved
  • Window venting improved is YES
  • Windows Improved section is YES if being improved and NO if not being improved
  • Window area mirrors Base area inputs
NOTE- Double pane windows cannot be improved, Only Single Pane windows

ExtraProperties.Name: Windows
ExtraProperties.Label: Windows
ExtraProperties.HelpText: Required photos- Close up of each window if improving , if not improving a wide shot of each side of home

ExtraProperties.Name: WaterHeaterHelp
ExtraProperties.Text:
Base
  • Water temp matches picture provided
  • Verify Model number input matches Picture provided
  • Verify Spec input information based on model number 
Improved
  • Water temp can only be improved by 20 degrees, lowest being 120 degrees
  • Verify improved model number matches AHRI picture
  • Verify improved Spec inputs matches AHRI provided

ExtraProperties.Name: WaterHeater
ExtraProperties.Label: Water Heater
ExtraProperties.HelpText: Required photos-Unit picture and Data Plate Picture

ExtraProperties.Name: SnugproReportHelp
ExtraProperties.Text:
• Once complete, notify contractors on any corrections needed (if any)
  • Verify Everything that is approved on Snuggpro matches MIR
  • Verify all pricing corresponds with approved measure 
  • Download and upload the Snuggpro report
